title: Skeptic Engine vs Existing Tools — Formal Comparison

comparison.method: Benford digit + cell-level + Isolation Forest fusion
comparison.unique_finding: Benford inversion — scRNA-seq UMI fd₁=0.74 vs expected 0.30

comparison.method: Library size, gene count, mito% thresholds
comparison.unique_finding: None — detects obvious noise but misses resample (-4.2pp vs fusion)
comparison.limitation: Designed for biological QC, not data integrity. Misses structure-preserving fabrication.

comparison.method: Benford digit analysis on copy-number data
comparison.unique_finding: First application of Benford to omics data
comparison.limitation: CNA only, no scRNA-seq. No fusion with structural features.

comparison.scope: Statistical reporting errors in published papers
comparison.unique_finding: Automated error detection at scale (688K articles)
comparison.limitation: Only detects APA-format reporting errors. Cannot analyze raw data.

comparison.scope: Publication bias / p-hacking detection in literature
comparison.limitation: Single aggregate statistic. H23 behavioral features beat it by +7.5pp on real data.

comparison.scope: P-hacking detection via behavioral sequence analysis
comparison.method: 18 p-value sequence features (fraud transfer from finance)
comparison.unique_finding: Behavioral sequence features from financial fraud detection transfer to p-hacking
comparison.limitation: Small sample (n=99). Needs replication on larger dataset.

comparison.unique_finding: AE catches structure-breaking fabrication that Benford misses (and vice versa)

key_differentiators: Only tool combining digit forensics + structural features + anomaly scoring (fusion)
Only tool demonstrating adversarial robustness (Benford-aware fabricator AUC=1.000)
Novel Benford inversion finding in scRNA-seq (not described in prior literature)
Honest documentation of cross-dataset failure (unique in the field)
Financial fraud detection → scientific integrity transfer (novel interdisciplinary bridge)
Full statistical robustness: 100 CV iterations, permutation tests, bootstrap CIs, Cohen's d
